questions.stimulus_text: Duckbill dinosaurs, like today's monitor lizards, had particularly long tails, which they could whip at considerable speed. Monitor lizards use their tails to strike predators. However, although duckbill tails were otherwise very similar to those of monitor lizards, the duckbill's tailbones were proportionately much thinner and thus more delicate. Moreover, to ward off their proportionately much larger predators, duckbills would have had to whip their tails considerably faster than monitor lizards do.
questions.question_text: The information given, if accurate, provides the strongest support for which of the following hypotheses?

options.a: If duckbills whipped their tails faster than monitor lizards do, the duckbill's tail would have been effective at warding off the duckbills' fiercest predators.
options.b: Duckbills used their tails to strike predators, and their tailbones were frequently damaged from the impact.
options.c: Using their tails was not the only means duckbills had for warding off predators.
options.d: Duckbills were at much greater risk of being killed by a predator than monitor lizards are.
options.e: The tails of duckbills, if used to ward off predators, would have been more likely than the tails of monitor lizards to sustain damage from the impact.

questions.stimulus_text: In an attempt to produce a coffee plant that would yield beans containing no caffeine, the synthesis of a substance known to be integral to the initial stages of caffeine production was blocked either in the beans, in the leaves, or both. For those plants in which synthesis of the substance was blocked only in the leaves, the resulting beans contained no caffeine.
questions.question_text: Any of the following, if true, would provide the basis for an explanation of the observed results EXCEPT:

options.a: In coffee plants, the substance is synthesized only in the leaves and then moves to the beans, where the initial stages of caffeine production take place.
options.b: In coffee plants, the last stage of caffeine production takes place in the beans using a compound that is produced only in the leaves by the substance.
options.c: In coffee plants, the initial stages of caffeine production take place only in the beans, but later stages depend on another substance that is synthesized only in the leaves and does not depend on the blocked substance.
options.d: In coffee plants, caffeine production takes place only in the leaves, but the caffeine then moves to the beans.
options.e: Caffeine was produced in the beans of the modified coffee plants, but all of it moved to the leaves, which normally produce their own caffeine.

questions.stimulus_text: Laminated glass is much harder to break than the glass typically used in the windows of cars driven in Relnia. It is more difficult for thieves to break into cars with laminated glass windows than into cars with ordinary glass windows, and laminated glass windows are less likely to break in a collision. Nevertheless, considerations of security and safety do not unambiguously support a proposal to require that in Relnia all glass installed in cars be laminated glass, since ________.
questions.question_text: Which of the following most logically completes the passage?

options.a: most people cannot visually distinguish laminated glass from the glass typically used for car windows
options.b: a significant proportion of cars driven in Relnia are manufactured elsewhere
options.c: some cars in Relnia already have laminated glass in their windows
options.d: the rates of car theft and of collisions have both fallen slightly in Relnia in recent years
options.e: there are times when breaking a car's window is the best way to provide timely help for people trapped inside
